summary: Clear soundboard capture of a hot late‑'87 show highlighted by a blistering Bucket > Fire, a powerful The Other One and an unexpected Terrapin surprise.
review: The three fan reviews paint this September‑8‑1987 Providence show as solid and energetic – the first set was “very hot,” while the second delivered a scorching Bucket > Fire transition, a leaping The Other One and a well‑played Terrapin Station. No recording flaws are mentioned, suggesting a clean soundboard source that lets the band’s tight, zippy playing shine through.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews consistently praise the performance (especially Bucket > Fire, The Other One and Terrapin) and note no audio problems; weighted average of show (70%) and recording (30%) yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No complaints about clarity, drop‑outs or mix balance were made, indicating a clear and listenable soundboard recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as very hot; second set featured a standout Bucket > Fire transition, an electrifying The Other One, a surprising Terrapin Station and a high‑energy Bobby closing rave-up.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned nostalgically (RIP Jer) but no specific comment on his playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby's closing rave‑up was highlighted as a happy, energetic finish.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent referenced in tribute; no performance detail provided.
